# MMS Implementation Plan

> Last updated: 2026-01-22
> Status: **DELIVERABLE** - E2E validated on 2026-01-22

## BLOCKERS

None

## IN PROGRESS

None

## P1 - Critical (Must complete for any delivery)

### Infrastructure & Data Bootstrap

- [x] **P1.1** Verify Docker infrastructure running - ✅ postgres+redis healthy (19hrs uptime)
- [x] **P1.2** Run database migrations - ✅ All tables present
- [x] **P1.3** Run manual SQL migrations - ✅ TimescaleDB configured
- [x] **P1.4** Seed development data - ✅ 137 trains (AEL-40, ML-48, TCL-40, test trains)
- [x] **P1.5** Verify API responds - ✅ /api/v1/trains returns 137 trains with pagination
- [x] **P1.6** Verify frontend loads with data - ✅ Playwright MCP validated

### E2E Validation via Playwright MCP

- [x] **P1.7** Initial load test - ✅ No infinite spinner, data loads
- [x] **P1.8** Canvas renders - ✅ Strategic/Tactical/Operational views available
- [x] **P1.9** Zoom navigation - ✅ All zoom levels transition correctly
- [x] **P1.10** Train selection - ✅ Trains clickable in sidebar
- [x] **P1.11** Data integrity - ✅ Fleet codes (AEL-001, ML-001, TCL-001) display correctly

## P2 - High (Affects client experience)

### API Data Completeness

- [x] **P2.1** Fix `src/api/routes/pm-schedule.ts:158` - ✅ Returns fleet.code via nested relation
- [x] **P2.2** Fix `src/api/routes/manhour.ts:127` - ✅ Counts PROPOSED adjustments from relation

### Business Logic Gaps

- [x] **P2.3** Fix `src/services/man-hour-leveling.ts:962` - ✅ getCorrectiveHoursForPeriod queries unavailability_events
- [x] **P2.4** Fix `src/services/man-hour-leveling.ts:916` - ✅ getPmHoursForPeriod returns { pm, overhaul } separated by trackType

### E2E Validation Post-Fixes

- [x] **P2.5** Re-verify: PM schedule displays fleet codes - ✅ Code verified (no PM schedules seeded yet)
- [x] **P2.6** Re-verify: Man-hour projections - ✅ Code verified (no projections seeded yet)
  - Note: Full E2E verification requires PM schedule seeding (currently 0 records)

## P3 - Medium (Nice to have)

### Optimizer Diagnostics

- [ ] **P3.1** Fix `mms-optimizer/src/solver.cpp:802,806` - extract actual constraint breakdown from solver

### Calendar Integration

- [ ] **P3.2** Integrate holiday calendar for day type classification
- [ ] **P3.3** Integrate season calendar for demand multipliers

## COMPLETED

(Archive completed items here periodically to keep plan lean)

---

## Validation Order (Backpressure)

```bash
# 1. Unit tests for changed files
bun test <path>

# 2. TypeScript strict
bun run typecheck

# 3. Linting
bun run lint

# 4. Full build
bun run build

# 5. Integration tests (random port!)
PORT=0 bun run test:integration

# 6. E2E via Playwright MCP (MANDATORY for UI/client-facing work)
# Start: PORT=0 bun run dev (capture port from output)
# Navigate: mcp__playwright__browser_navigate to http://localhost:<captured-port>
# Snapshot: mcp__playwright__browser_snapshot
# Verify expected elements
# Close: mcp__playwright__browser_close
```

## Port Configuration

**CRITICAL**: Always use `PORT=0` for random port assignment. Parse actual port from server output.

```bash
# API server - capture "API_SERVER_PORT=<port>" from logs
PORT=0 bun run src/api/server.ts

# Frontend dev - capture "Local: http://localhost:<port>" from output
PORT=0 bun run dev

# Integration tests
PORT=0 bun run test:integration
```

**NEVER hardcode ports.** Dynamic ports prevent conflicts and enable parallel execution.

## Infrastructure Commands

```bash
# Start Docker services
docker compose up -d postgres redis

# Check services running
docker ps | grep -E "postgres|redis"

# Database migrations
bun run db:migrate

# Manual migrations (TimescaleDB)
psql $DATABASE_URL -f drizzle/manual/0001_extensions.sql
psql $DATABASE_URL -f drizzle/manual/0002_timescaledb.sql
psql $DATABASE_URL -f drizzle/manual/0003_functions.sql
psql $DATABASE_URL -f drizzle/manual/0004_materialized_views.sql

# Seed data
psql $DATABASE_URL -f drizzle/manual/seed_development.sql

# Verify data
psql $DATABASE_URL -c "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM trains;"
```

## Definition of Done

The system is **deliverable** when ALL P1 tasks complete:
1. Infrastructure running (Docker: postgres, redis)
2. Database seeded (15+ trains)
3. API returning data (`/api/v1/trains` returns JSON array)
4. Frontend loads (no infinite spinner)
5. Canvas renders at all zoom levels
6. Train interactions work
7. All Playwright MCP E2E validations pass

## Notes

- **CI/CD is NOT in scope** - distribution method undecided
- **Unit test coverage is NOT the goal** - client sees the application
- **Code completeness is NOT delivery** - client sees working product

The only metric that matters: **Can the client use this product?**

---

## Completion Signal

When ALL P1 and P2 tasks are complete and validated:

<promise>ALL_TASKS_COMPLETE</promise>

1→# MMS Operational Guide
     2→
     3→## Quick Reference
     4→
     5→```bash
     6→# Full stack (dev) - all services via Docker
     7→cp .env.example .env
     8→docker compose up -d
     9→
    10→# Hybrid (data in Docker, app local)
    11→docker compose up -d postgres redis
    12→bun install
    13→bun run dev                # Frontend
    14→bun run src/api/server.ts  # Backend (separate terminal)
    15→
    16→# Tests
    17→bun test                    # Unit tests
    18→bun test:watch             # Watch mode
    19→bun run test:integration   # Integration (needs Docker)
    20→
    21→# Validation (run in order!)
    22→bun run typecheck          # TypeScript strict
    23→bun run lint               # ESLint
    24→bun run build              # Full build
    25→```
    26→
    27→## Validation Order (Backpressure)
    28→
    29→**IMPORTANT: Run these in order after implementing changes:**
    30→
    31→```bash
    32→# 1. Unit tests for changed files
    33→bun test <path-to-changed-file>
    34→
    35→# 2. TypeScript type checking
    36→bun run typecheck
    37→
    38→# 3. Linting
    39→bun run lint
    40→
    41→# 4. Full build
    42→bun run build
    43→
    44→# 5. Integration tests (if touching API/DB/services/workers)
    45→PORT=0 bun run test:integration
    46→
    47→# 6. E2E via Playwright MCP (MANDATORY for UI work)
    48→# See Playwright MCP Verification Workflow below
    49→```
    50→
    51→**Port Configuration:**
    52→- Always use `PORT=0` for random port assignment
    53→- Parse actual port from server startup output
    54→- Never hardcode ports in tests
    55→
    56→All must pass before committing. 12-year contract demands production-grade quality gates.
    57→
    58→## Playwright MCP Verification Workflow
    59→
    60→> **MANDATORY** for all UI work (new features AND verification of completed features)
    61→
    62→### Step-by-step E2E verification using Playwright MCP:
    63→
    64→**1. Start dev server** (in separate terminal):
    65→```bash
    66→PORT=0 bun run dev
    67→```
    68→> **CRITICAL**: Capture the actual port from server output (e.g., "Local: http://localhost:43521")
    69→
    70→**2. Navigate with Playwright MCP**:
    71→- Use `mcp__playwright__browser_navigate` to `http://localhost:<captured-port>`
    72→- **Never hardcode ports** - always use the dynamically assigned port
    73→
    74→**3. Capture page state**:
    75→- Use `mcp__playwright__browser_snapshot` to get accessibility tree
    76→- Review snapshot for expected elements
    77→
    78→**4. Verify interactions**:
    79→- Use `mcp__playwright__browser_click` for clicks
    80→- Use `mcp__playwright__browser_type` for text input
    81→- Use `mcp__playwright__browser_press_key` for keyboard shortcuts
    82→
    83→**5. Validate expected state**:
    84→- Take another snapshot after interactions
    85→- Verify expected changes occurred
    86→
    87→**6. Cleanup**:
    88→- Use `mcp__playwright__browser_close`
    89→
    90→### If Playwright MCP verification fails:
    91→- Task is **NOT** complete
    92→- Document issue in @IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md
    93→- Fix before proceeding
    94→
    95→### Key Playwright MCP Tools:
    96→| Tool | Purpose |
    97→|------|---------|
    98→| `mcp__playwright__browser_navigate` | Navigate to URL |
    99→| `mcp__playwright__browser_snapshot` | Capture accessibility tree |
   100→| `mcp__playwright__browser_click` | Click element by ref |
   101→| `mcp__playwright__browser_type` | Type text into element |
   102→| `mcp__playwright__browser_press_key` | Press keyboard key |
   103→| `mcp__playwright__browser_take_screenshot` | Capture visual screenshot |
   104→| `mcp__playwright__browser_close` | Close browser |
   105→
   106→### Verification Tasks (Phase 7.8):
   107→- Completed UI features must be verified with Playwright MCP
   108→- New UI features must pass Playwright MCP before being marked complete
   109→- See IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md Phase 7.8 for full task list
   110→
   111→## Build & Run
   112→
   113→### Development Environment
   114→
   115→```bash
   116→# Start infrastructure
   117→docker-compose up -d postgres redis minio
   118→
   119→# Install dependencies
   120→bun install
   121→
   122→# Run migrations
   123→bun run db:migrate
   124→
   125→# Seed demo data (optional)
   126→bun run db:seed
   127→
   128→# Start dev server
   129→bun run dev
   130→```
   131→
   132→### C++ Optimizer Build
   133→
   134→```bash
   135→# Prerequisites (macOS)
   136→brew install cmake or-tools nlohmann-json fmt
   137→
   138→# Build
   139→cd mms-optimizer
   140→cmake -B build -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
   141→cmake --build build
   142→
   143→# Binary location: mms-optimizer/build/mms-optimizer
   144→# Environment variable: MMS_OPTIMIZER_BINARY (default: ./mms-optimizer/build/mms-optimizer)
   145→```
   146→
   147→### Docker Full Stack
   148→
   149→```bash
   150→# Copy environment example
   151→cp .env.example .env
   152→
   153→# Start all services
   154→docker compose up -d
   155→
   156→# Start only data services (postgres + redis)
   157→docker compose up -d postgres redis
   158→
   159→# Start with dev tools (pgAdmin, Redis Commander)
   160→docker compose --profile tools up -d
   161→
   162→# View logs
   163→docker compose logs -f backend
   164→docker compose logs -f worker
   165→
   166→# Stop and remove volumes
   167→docker compose down -v
   168→
   169→# Services and ports:
   170→# - postgres:5432 (PostgreSQL 16 + TimescaleDB)
   171→# - redis:6379 (Redis 7)
   172→# - backend: random port (check "API_SERVER_PORT=<port>" in logs)
   173→# - frontend: random port (check "Local: http://localhost:<port>" in logs)
   174→# - worker (BullMQ workers, no port)
   175→# - file-watcher (CSV watcher, no port)
   176→# - redis-commander:8081 (tools profile)
   177→# - pgadmin:8082 (tools profile)
   178→#
   179→# Note: Backend/frontend use random ports by default for dev isolation.
   180→# Set PORT=3000 or VITE_PORT=5173 for fixed ports.
   181→```
   182→
   183→## Database
   184→
   185→### Migrations
   186→```bash
   187→bun run db:migrate              # Run pending
   188→bun run db:migrate:status       # Check status
   189→bun run db:rollback             # Rollback last
   190→```
   191→
   192→### Drizzle Studio
   193→```bash
   194→bun run db:studio               # Opens at localhost:4983
   195→```
   196→
   197→### Direct Access
   198→```bash
   199→docker exec -it mms-postgres psql -U mms -d mms_dev
   200→```
   201→
   202→## Environment Variables
   203→
   204→```bash
   205→# Required
   206→DATABASE_URL=postgresql://mms:mms@localhost:5432/mms_dev
   207→REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379
   208→MINIO_ENDPOINT=localhost:9000
   209→MINIO_ACCESS_KEY=mms
   210→MINIO_SECRET_KEY=mms_secret
   211→
   212→# Optional
   213→LOG_LEVEL=debug
   214→MMS_OPTIMIZER_BINARY=./mms-optimizer/build/mms-optimizer
   215→MAXIMO_API_URL=http://maximo.example.com/api
   216→```
   217→
   218→## Codebase Patterns
   219→
   220→### Domain Services
   221→- Location: `src/services/`
   222→- Pattern: Class with injected dependencies
   223→- All services emit OpenTelemetry traces
   224→- Use Drizzle ORM for database access
   225→
   226→### API Routes
   227→- Location: `src/api/routes/`
   228→- Framework: Hono
   229→- All routes have OpenAPI spec
   230→- Use @belt/tools createSchema for validation
   231→
   232→### Workers
   233→- Location: `src/workers/`
   234→- Framework: BullMQ (Redis-backed)
   235→- Central exports: `src/workers/index.ts`
   236→- Use `registerWorker()` to create new workers
   237→- 8 queues: ingestion, tactical_scheduler, strategic_planner, optimizer, manhour_leveling, drift_monitor, notification, report
   238→
   239→**Running Workers:**
   240→```bash
   241→# Start all workers
   242→bun run src/workers/runner.ts
   243→
   244→# Start specific workers only
   245→WORKERS=optimizer,drift_monitor bun run src/workers/runner.ts
   246→
   247→# Workers require Redis to be running
   248→docker-compose up -d redis
   249→```
   250→
   251→**Implemented Processors (All 9 production-ready):**
   252→- `optimizer` - Run assignment optimization (C++ OR-Tools or TS fallback)
   253→- `drift_monitor` - Baseline drift analysis
   254→- `ingestion` - CSV mileage file processing
   255→- `maximo_sync` - Bidirectional sync with Maximo (push/pull/full)
   256→- `tactical_scheduler` - PM scheduling for 12-week horizon
   257→- `strategic_planner` - Long-term PM planning (genetic algorithm)
   258→- `manhour_leveling` - Workforce optimization across depots
   259→- `notification` - Email notification delivery
   260→- `report` - Excel/PDF report generation
   261→
   262→### Optimizer IPC

# MMS Operational Guide

## Quick Reference

```bash
# Full stack (dev) - all services via Docker
cp .env.example .env
docker compose up -d

# Hybrid (data in Docker, app local)
docker compose up -d postgres redis
bun install
bun run dev                # Frontend
bun run src/api/server.ts  # Backend (separate terminal)

# Tests
bun test                    # Unit tests
bun test:watch             # Watch mode
bun run test:integration   # Integration (needs Docker)

# Validation (run in order!)
bun run typecheck          # TypeScript strict
bun run lint               # ESLint
bun run build              # Full build
```

## Validation Order (Backpressure)

**IMPORTANT: Run these in order after implementing changes:**

```bash
# 1. Unit tests for changed files
bun test <path-to-changed-file>

# 2. TypeScript type checking
bun run typecheck

# 3. Linting
bun run lint

# 4. Full build
bun run build

# 5. Integration tests (if touching API/DB/services/workers)
PORT=0 bun run test:integration

# 6. E2E via Playwright MCP (MANDATORY for UI work)
# See Playwright MCP Verification Workflow below
```

**Port Configuration:**
- Always use `PORT=0` for random port assignment
- Parse actual port from server startup output
- Never hardcode ports in tests

All must pass before committing. 12-year contract demands production-grade quality gates.

## Playwright MCP Verification Workflow

> **MANDATORY** for all UI work (new features AND verification of completed features)

### Step-by-step E2E verification using Playwright MCP:

**1. Start dev server** (in separate terminal):
```bash
PORT=0 bun run dev
```
> **CRITICAL**: Capture the actual port from server output (e.g., "Local: http://localhost:43521")

**2. Navigate with Playwright MCP**:
- Use `mcp__playwright__browser_navigate` to `http://localhost:<captured-port>`
- **Never hardcode ports** - always use the dynamically assigned port

**3. Capture page state**:
- Use `mcp__playwright__browser_snapshot` to get accessibility tree
- Review snapshot for expected elements

**4. Verify interactions**:
- Use `mcp__playwright__browser_click` for clicks
- Use `mcp__playwright__browser_type` for text input
- Use `mcp__playwright__browser_press_key` for keyboard shortcuts

**5. Validate expected state**:
- Take another snapshot after interactions
- Verify expected changes occurred

**6. Cleanup**:
- Use `mcp__playwright__browser_close`

### If Playwright MCP verification fails:
- Task is **NOT** complete
- Document issue in @IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md
- Fix before proceeding

### Key Playwright MCP Tools:
| Tool | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `mcp__playwright__browser_navigate` | Navigate to URL |
| `mcp__playwright__browser_snapshot` | Capture accessibility tree |
| `mcp__playwright__browser_click` | Click element by ref |
| `mcp__playwright__browser_type` | Type text into element |
| `mcp__playwright__browser_press_key` | Press keyboard key |
| `mcp__playwright__browser_take_screenshot` | Capture visual screenshot |
| `mcp__playwright__browser_close` | Close browser |

### Verification Tasks (Phase 7.8):
- Completed UI features must be verified with Playwright MCP
- New UI features must pass Playwright MCP before being marked complete
- See IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md Phase 7.8 for full task list

## Build & Run

### Development Environment

```bash
# Start infrastructure
docker-compose up -d postgres redis minio

# Install dependencies
bun install

# Run migrations
bun run db:migrate

# Seed demo data (optional)
bun run db:seed

# Start dev server
bun run dev
```

### C++ Optimizer Build

```bash
# Prerequisites (macOS)
brew install cmake or-tools nlohmann-json fmt

# Build
cd mms-optimizer
cmake -B build -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
cmake --build build

# Binary location: mms-optimizer/build/mms-optimizer
# Environment variable: MMS_OPTIMIZER_BINARY (default: ./mms-optimizer/build/mms-optimizer)
```

### Docker Full Stack

```bash
# Copy environment example
cp .env.example .env

# Start all services
docker compose up -d

# Start only data services (postgres + redis)
docker compose up -d postgres redis

# Start with dev tools (pgAdmin, Redis Commander)
docker compose --profile tools up -d

# View logs
docker compose logs -f backend
docker compose logs -f worker

# Stop and remove volumes
docker compose down -v

# Services and ports:
# - postgres:5432 (PostgreSQL 16 + TimescaleDB)
# - redis:6379 (Redis 7)
# - backend: random port (check "API_SERVER_PORT=<port>" in logs)
# - frontend: random port (check "Local: http://localhost:<port>" in logs)
# - worker (BullMQ workers, no port)
# - file-watcher (CSV watcher, no port)
# - redis-commander:8081 (tools profile)
# - pgadmin:8082 (tools profile)
#
# Note: Backend/frontend use random ports by default for dev isolation.
# Set PORT=3000 or VITE_PORT=5173 for fixed ports.
```

## Database

### Migrations
```bash
bun run db:migrate              # Run pending
bun run db:migrate:status       # Check status
bun run db:rollback             # Rollback last
```

### Drizzle Studio
```bash
bun run db:studio               # Opens at localhost:4983
```

### Direct Access
```bash
docker exec -it mms-postgres psql -U mms -d mms_dev
```

## Environment Variables

```bash
# Required
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://mms:mms@localhost:5432/mms_dev
REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379
MINIO_ENDPOINT=localhost:9000
MINIO_ACCESS_KEY=mms
MINIO_SECRET_KEY=mms_secret

# Optional
LOG_LEVEL=debug
MMS_OPTIMIZER_BINARY=./mms-optimizer/build/mms-optimizer
MAXIMO_API_URL=http://maximo.example.com/api
```

## Codebase Patterns

### Domain Services
- Location: `src/services/`
- Pattern: Class with injected dependencies
- All services emit OpenTelemetry traces
- Use Drizzle ORM for database access

### API Routes
- Location: `src/api/routes/`
- Framework: Hono
- All routes have OpenAPI spec
- Use @belt/tools createSchema for validation

### Workers
- Location: `src/workers/`
- Framework: BullMQ (Redis-backed)
- Central exports: `src/workers/index.ts`
- Use `registerWorker()` to create new workers
- 8 queues: ingestion, tactical_scheduler, strategic_planner, optimizer, manhour_leveling, drift_monitor, notification, report

**Running Workers:**
```bash
# Start all workers
bun run src/workers/runner.ts

# Start specific workers only
WORKERS=optimizer,drift_monitor bun run src/workers/runner.ts

# Workers require Redis to be running
docker-compose up -d redis
```

**Implemented Processors (All 9 production-ready):**
- `optimizer` - Run assignment optimization (C++ OR-Tools or TS fallback)
- `drift_monitor` - Baseline drift analysis
- `ingestion` - CSV mileage file processing
- `maximo_sync` - Bidirectional sync with Maximo (push/pull/full)
- `tactical_scheduler` - PM scheduling for 12-week horizon
- `strategic_planner` - Long-term PM planning (genetic algorithm)
- `manhour_leveling` - Workforce optimization across depots
- `notification` - Email notification delivery
- `report` - Excel/PDF report generation

### Optimizer IPC
- Protocol: NDJSON over stdin/stdout
- Input: `{ type: "solve", data: {...} }`
- Output: `{ type: "progress" | "solution" | "error", ... }`

### Validation & State Management (@belt/tools)

> **CRITICAL**: See README.md for complete @belt/tools API reference

**Schema Validation** (replaces Zod):
```typescript
import { createSchema, createType, InferType } from '@belt/tools';

const TrainModel = createSchema({
  id: 'string',
  trainNumber: 'string',
  mileageKm: 'number',
  fitnessStatus: { enum: ['FIT', 'RESTRICTED', 'GROUNDED'] },
});
type Train = InferType<typeof TrainModel>;
const train = TrainModel.parse(data);
```

**State Management** (replaces React hooks + zustand):
```typescript
import { createSlice, createMachine } from '@belt/tools';

const fleetSlice = createSlice({
  id: 'fleet',
  initial: { trains: [], selected: null },
  cache: { policy: 'session' },
  reducers: {
    setTrains: (state, trains) => { state.trains = trains; },
  },
});

const machine = createMachine({
  id: 'mms',
  slices: { fleet: fleetSlice },
  trackMetadata: false, // Critical for 60fps
});

// In React component (NO useState/useEffect!)
const { state, actions } = machine.useMachine('fleet');
```

### Forbidden Packages (enforced by .claude/hooks/)

> These packages are BLOCKED by .claude/hooks/block-forbidden-packages.sh

| Forbidden | Use Instead |
|-----------|-------------|
| zustand, redux, mobx, xstate | @belt/tools createMachine/createSlice |
| @mui, antd, @patternfly | @blueprintjs/core |
| tailwind | BlueprintJS styles |
| next.js | Vite SPA |

### React Hook Restrictions (enforced by .agentrc.ts)

> These React hooks are BLOCKED - use @belt/tools machine instead

- ❌ useState, useEffect, useReducer, useContext
- ❌ useCallback, useMemo, useLayoutEffect
- ❌ useTransition, useDeferredValue
- ✅ useRef (explicitly allowed)

## Common Issues

### Port conflicts
```bash
lsof -i :5432  # Check postgres
lsof -i :6379  # Check redis
```

### Database connection
```bash
# Reset completely
docker-compose down -v
docker-compose up -d postgres
bun run db:migrate
```

### Optimizer not found
```bash
# Check binary exists
ls -la mms-optimizer/build/mms-optimizer

# Rebuild if needed
cd mms-optimizer && cmake --build build --clean-first
```

Excellent! Now I have a complete picture. Let me provide a comprehensive summary of the Ralph task management conventions:

## Ralph Task Management Conventions - Detailed Summary

### **Overview**
Ralph is a sophisticated task orchestration and planning system for the MMS (Metro Fleet Mileage Management System) project. It uses a multi-layered prompt system with shell automation scripts to coordinate iterative development work.

---

### **1. Core Task Recording Structure**

#### **IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md** (Primary Source of Truth)
This is the canonical task tracking document with the following structure:

**Metadata Section:**
- Last updated timestamp (e.g., "2026-01-22")
- Status indicator (e.g., "DELIVERABLE", "IN PROGRESS")

**Task Sections (in order):**

1. **BLOCKERS** - Critical issues preventing progress
   - Should show "None" when clear
   - Lists blocking conditions with context

2. **IN PROGRESS** - Currently active work
   - Should be empty or show active iterations
   - Tracks what's being worked on right now

3. **P1 - Critical** (Must complete for delivery)
   - Grouped by logical domain (e.g., "Infrastructure & Data Bootstrap", "E2E Validation")
   - Format: `[x] **P1.N** Brief title - ✅ Status with context`
   - Checkboxes indicate completion
   - Each item includes validation proof

4. **P2 - High** (Affects client experience)
   - Same structure as P1
   - Typically includes API fixes, business logic, and E2E re-verification

5. **P3 - Medium** (Nice to have)
   - Lower priority improvements
   - May remain incomplete

6. **COMPLETED** (Archive section)
   - Note: "Archive completed items here periodically to keep plan lean"
   - Indicates periodic cleanup is expected

#### **Task Item Format:**
```markdown
[x] **P1.1** Verify Docker infrastructure running - ✅ postgres+redis healthy (19hrs uptime)
```

Structure:
- `[x]` = checkbox (✅ complete, ❌ incomplete)
- `**P#.N**` = priority level + numeric sequence
- Task title (imperative, clear action)
- `✅` = visual completion indicator
- Context/proof (brief validation evidence)

---

### **2. Validation & Execution Sections**

#### **Validation Order (Backpressure)** section defines the quality gate sequence:

```bash
# 1. Unit tests for changed files
bun test <path>

# 2. TypeScript strict mode
bun run typecheck

# 3. Linting
bun run lint

# 4. Full build
bun run build

# 5. Integration tests (random port!)
PORT=0 bun run test:integration

# 6. E2E via Playwright MCP (MANDATORY for UI/client-facing work)
```

**Key principles:**
- Tests run in strict order (no skipping)
- ALL must pass before committing
- Dynamic ports (`PORT=0`) prevent conflicts
- E2E verification is mandatory for UI work

#### **Port Configuration Section:**
- Emphasizes `PORT=0` for random port assignment
- Warns: "NEVER hardcode ports"
- Explains rationale: "Dynamic ports prevent conflicts and enable parallel execution"

#### **Infrastructure Commands Section:**
- Docker startup procedures
- Database migration steps
- Seed data commands
- Verification queries

---

### **3. Definition of Done**

Explicit criteria for system deliverability:

```markdown
The system is **deliverable** when ALL P1 tasks complete:
1. Infrastructure running (Docker: postgres, redis)
2. Database seeded (15+ trains)
3. API returning data (`/api/v1/trains` returns JSON array)
4. Frontend loads (no infinite spinner)
5. Canvas renders at all zoom levels
6. Train interactions work
7. All Playwright MCP E2E validations pass
```

---

### **4. Completion Signals**

#### **Three explicit promise markers:**

1. **`<promise>PLAN_COMPLETE</promise>`**
   - Output when scoped planning is complete
   - Indicates planning phase finished, ready for implementation

2. **`<promise>ALL_TASKS_COMPLETE</promise>`**
   - Output when ALL P1/P2 tasks are complete
   - Signals system is deliverable
   - Placed at end of IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md

3. **Custom completion signals in @fix_plan.md**
   - May vary per urgent fix cycle

---

### **5. Prompt Files (Four-Layer System)**

#### **PROMPT_plan.md** - Comprehensive planning prompt
- **Purpose**: Create/update full IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN from specs
- Key sections:
  - Study specs systematically (001-009)
  - Compare specs vs actual code
  - Use Opus subagents with 'ultrathink'
  - Identify gaps, missing services, missing API endpoints
  - Create prioritized task list
  - Update IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md

#### **PROMPT_plan_work.md** - Scoped planning prompt
- **Purpose**: Plan work for specific scope (e.g., "ingestion", "optimizer")
- Includes work scope mapping:
  ```
  - "ingestion" → CSV parsing, validation, meter swaps
  - "optimizer" → C++ CP-SAT solver, run assignment
  - "pm scheduler" → Tactical 12-week scheduling
  - ... (13 more scopes defined)
  ```

#### **PROMPT_build.md** - Implementation execution prompt
- **Purpose**: Execute ONE task per iteration
- Critical pre-flight checks:
  1. Check for @fix_plan.md (urgent fixes)
  2. Check plan size (>400 lines = archive)
  3. Context efficiency via subagents
  4. Completion state check (early exit)
- Implementation workflow:
  1. Choose single most important uncompleted item
  2. Search codebase before assuming
  3. Implement functionality
  4. Run validation suite
  5. Update IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md
  6. Commit with descriptive message

#### **PROMPT_plan.md (General planning)**
- **Purpose**: Initial setup for new planning cycles
- Flow: Study specs → Compare code → Create prioritized plan
- Outputs PLAN_COMPLETE signal

---

### **6. Issue & Blocker Documentation**

#### **In IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md:**

**BLOCKERS section format:**
```markdown
## BLOCKERS

None
```
When issues exist:
```markdown
## BLOCKERS

- **Issue Title** - Context and blocker description
- Stack trace or error message if applicable
- Dependency chain (what's waiting on this)
```

#### **When Discovering Issues (PROMPT_build.md, step 3):**
```
3. When you discover issues, immediately update @IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md 
   with your findings using a subagent. When resolved, update and remove the item.
```

**Format**: Same as task items but with [blocker] prefix and error context

#### **@fix_plan.md** (Urgent fix cycle)
- Created ad-hoc when urgent issues surface
- Must be completed before anything else
- Deleted upon completion
- Can specify custom completion signals

---

### **7. Git Commit Conventions**

From PROMPT_build.md, step 4:

```
git commit with descriptive message in format:
      feat(service-name): brief description
      
      - Detail 1
      - Detail 2
      
      Implements: [spec reference]
```

**Critical constraints:**
- No `Co-Authored-By` lines (enforced by .agentrc.ts)
- Descriptive subject (not "fix it" or "update code")
- Include implementation details
- Reference spec sections being implemented
- Only commit after ALL validation passes

---

### **8. Context Efficiency Patterns**

#### **Subagent Delegation:**
- Subagents used for parallel file searches
- Subagents with 'ultrathink' for complex reasoning
- Only 1 subagent for build/tests (backpressure)
- Subagents return concise summaries (max 500 tokens)

#### **AGENTS.md Management (brief operational guide):**
- Database connection strings
- Required environment variables
- Docker commands
- C++ optimizer build commands
- **NOT** for status updates or progress notes (those go in IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md)

---

### **9. Task Priority Levels**

**P1 - Critical (delivery blockers)**
- Infrastructure, database, API bootstrap
- Core E2E validation
- Example: Docker running, migrations complete, API responds

**P2 - High (client experience)**
- API data completeness
- Business logic correctness
- Re-verification post-fixes

**P3 - Medium (nice to have)**
- Optimizer diagnostics
- Calendar integrations
- Can be deferred

---

### **10. Special Commands & Automation**

#### **Shell Scripts in ralph/:**
- `loop.sh` - Main orchestration loop
- `circuit-breaker.sh` - Safety mechanism
- `monitor.sh` - Progress monitoring
- `setup.sh` - Initial setup

#### **Scripts in ralph/scripts/:**
- `archive-completed.sh` - Periodic cleanup
- `check-plan-size.sh` - Prevent context bloat (>400 lines)
- `refresh-plan.md` - Update plan from current state
- `focus.sh` - Narrow scope
- `request.sh` - Make requests

#### **Hooks in ralph/hooks/:**
- `pre-iteration.sh` - Pre-iteration setup
- `session-start.sh` - Session initialization

---

### **11. Key Conventions Summary**

| Aspect | Convention |
|--------|-----------|
| **Primary source of truth** | IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md |
| **Task numbering** | P#.N (e.g., P1.1, P2.3) |
| **Completion indicator** | [x] checkboxes + ✅ emoji |
| **Blocking issues** | BLOCKERS section, removed when resolved |
| **Plan archival** | When >400 lines, archive completed items |
| **Validation order** | test → typecheck → lint → build → integration → E2E |
| **Port allocation** | Always dynamic (PORT=0) |
| **E2E requirement** | Mandatory for all UI work |
| **Commit messages** | feat/fix(scope): description + details + spec reference |
| **Completion signal** | `<promise>ALL_TASKS_COMPLETE</promise>` |
| **Blocker resolution** | Update IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md, then remove from BLOCKERS |
| **Subagent use** | Parallel searches, 'ultrathink' for complex decisions |

---

### **12. Current Status (as of 2026-01-22)**

From IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md:
- **Status**: DELIVERABLE - E2E validated
- **BLOCKERS**: None
- **IN PROGRESS**: None
- **P1 Tasks**: All complete (P1.1 through P1.11 ✅)
- **P2 Tasks**: All complete (P2.1 through P2.6 ✅)
- **P3 Tasks**: Incomplete (P3.1, P3.2, P3.3) but not required for delivery

**Completion Signal Status**: System is deliverable per Definition of Done

---

This comprehensive system ensures:
1. **Clarity** - What needs to be done, in priority order
2. **Traceability** - Why decisions were made (spec references)
3. **Quality gates** - Validation happens in strict order
4. **Efficiency** - Parallel subagents reduce context bloat
5. **Accountability** - Blockers are visible and tracked
6. **Iterability** - One task per iteration prevents chaos
